title: Install plan shows per-client next steps for file-based MCP clients
content: Non-CLI clients (cursor, windsurf, vscode, gemini, kiro, cline, continue, zed, jetbrains) cannot auto-register MCP. The install plan prints a 'Next steps' section with client-specific restart/reload instructions. JetBrains gets a manual UI path since no config file is written for it. Defined in clientNextSteps map in src/cli/commands/install.ts.

body.text: Non-CLI clients (cursor, windsurf, vscode, gemini, kiro, cline, continue, zed, jetbrains) cannot auto-register MCP. The install plan prints a 'Next steps' section with client-specific restart/reload instructions. JetBrains gets a manual UI path since no config file is written for it. Defined in clientNextSteps map in src/cli/commands/install.ts.

title: Injected client instructions include three knowledge guardrail rules
content:
buildInstructionBlock() in src/install/installer.ts appends three guardrail bullets to every injected instruction block:
- Never store sensitive information such as credentials, API keys, tokens, or personal data.
- Never store proprietary or closed-source implementation details when working in an open-source project context.
- Only store knowledge directly relevant to the current project — skip observations about unrelated systems or codebases.

body.text:
buildInstructionBlock() in src/install/installer.ts appends three guardrail bullets to every injected instruction block:
- Never store sensitive information such as credentials, API keys, tokens, or personal data.
- Never store proprietary or closed-source implementation details when working in an open-source project context.
- Only store knowledge directly relevant to the current project — skip observations about unrelated systems or codebases.

title: Source provider question removed from install flow — defaults to local
content: The 'Where should Knowit store long-term memory?' prompt was removed. Source provider defaults to 'local' silently. The --source flag still works for Notion. Reason: exposing storage routing internals conflicts with the business model.

body.text: The 'Where should Knowit store long-term memory?' prompt was removed. Source provider defaults to 'local' silently. The --source flag still works for Notion. Reason: exposing storage routing internals conflicts with the business model.

title: Client selector is a multiselect with detection hints and pre-selection
content: During install, each supported client is probed via hasCommand() (binary on PATH) and dirExists() (known config/app dirs). Each option shows 'detected' or 'not found' as a hint. Claude, Codex, and Cursor are pre-selected if detected. All detection logic is in detectClient() in src/cli/commands/install.ts.

body.text: During install, each supported client is probed via hasCommand() (binary on PATH) and dirExists() (known config/app dirs). Each option shows 'detected' or 'not found' as a hint. Claude, Codex, and Cursor are pre-selected if detected. All detection logic is in detectClient() in src/cli/commands/install.ts.

title: Installation CLI uses @clack/prompts + picocolors
content: The install command uses @clack/prompts for interactive prompts and picocolors for terminal color output. No readline or inquirer. Clack provides select, multiselect, confirm, text, intro, outro, note, isCancel, cancel primitives.

body.text: The install command uses @clack/prompts for interactive prompts and picocolors for terminal color output. No readline or inquirer. Clack provides select, multiselect, confirm, text, intro, outro, note, isCancel, cancel primitives.

title: npx knowit install is the primary entry point
content: The primary install entry point is `npx knowit install`, not `npm install -g knowit` followed by `knowit install`. The wizard detects whether knowit is globally installed (via `which`/`where`) and asks the user if they want to install globally for faster MCP startup. If yes, it runs `npm install -g knowit` first, then configures MCP with `knowit serve`. If no (or skipped), MCP config uses `npx knowit serve` instead. The `useNpxForMcp` flag in InstallSelections/InstallPlan controls which command goes into .mcp.json and `claude mcp add` args.

body.text: The primary install entry point is `npx knowit install`, not `npm install -g knowit` followed by `knowit install`. The wizard detects whether knowit is globally installed (via `which`/`where`) and asks the user if they want to install globally for faster MCP startup. If yes, it runs `npm install -g knowit` first, then configures MCP with `knowit serve`. If no (or skipped), MCP config uses `npx knowit serve` instead. The `useNpxForMcp` flag in InstallSelections/InstallPlan controls which command goes into .mcp.json and `claude mcp add` args.

title: Cloud CLI command tree stayed on 0.2.x release line
content: Although the cloud CLI command tree changed from top-level auth commands to `knowit cloud ...`, the repo kept the release on the `0.2.x` line and used `0.2.6` instead of a minor bump. The rationale is that cloud commands are still gated off and not treated as an active public contract for current users.

body.text: Although the cloud CLI command tree changed from top-level auth commands to `knowit cloud ...`, the repo kept the release on the `0.2.x` line and used `0.2.6` instead of a minor bump. The rationale is that cloud commands are still gated off and not treated as an active public contract for current users.

title: Pre-1.0 CLI breaking changes use minor version bumps
content: When Knowit is still on `0.x`, a non-backward-compatible CLI surface change should bump the minor version rather than the patch version. The `knowit login` -> `knowit cloud login` command tree change was released as `0.3.0`, not `0.2.6`, because it changes the public CLI contract even though the cloud feature is currently gated off.

body.text: When Knowit is still on `0.x`, a non-backward-compatible CLI surface change should bump the minor version rather than the patch version. The `knowit login` -> `knowit cloud login` command tree change was released as `0.3.0`, not `0.2.6`, because it changes the public CLI contract even though the cloud feature is currently gated off.

title: Cloud CLI commands are grouped under knowit cloud
content: Cloud-facing CLI authentication commands are organized only under the `knowit cloud` namespace (`knowit cloud login`, `knowit cloud whoami`, `knowit cloud logout`) to match the existing grouped command style used by `knowit source`. Top-level `knowit login`, `knowit whoami`, and `knowit logout` aliases are not registered, because cloud access is still gated off and there is not enough active usage to justify carrying compatibility-only command aliases.

body.text: Cloud-facing CLI authentication commands are organized only under the `knowit cloud` namespace (`knowit cloud login`, `knowit cloud whoami`, `knowit cloud logout`) to match the existing grouped command style used by `knowit source`. Top-level `knowit login`, `knowit whoami`, and `knowit logout` aliases are not registered, because cloud access is still gated off and there is not enough active usage to justify carrying compatibility-only command aliases.

title: Cloud bootstrap honors explicit cloud-default preference and otherwise preserves local-first behavior
content: MemoryService bootstrap registers the cloud source when credentials or env tokens are available, but it only promotes cloud to default when an explicit preference is present (credentials defaultToCloud or KNOWIT_CLOUD_DEFAULT=true for tests/manual overrides). Without that preference, local remains or is restored as the default source.

body.text: MemoryService bootstrap registers the cloud source when credentials or env tokens are available, but it only promotes cloud to default when an explicit preference is present (credentials defaultToCloud or KNOWIT_CLOUD_DEFAULT=true for tests/manual overrides). Without that preference, local remains or is restored as the default source.

title: Logout removes stale cloud source state and restores local default
content: Logout now clears credentials, restores local SQLite as the default source, and removes the cloud source from the source registry so stale persisted cloud tokens do not remain available through the local DB after logout.

body.text: Logout now clears credentials, restores local SQLite as the default source, and removes the cloud source from the source registry so stale persisted cloud tokens do not remain available through the local DB after logout.

title: Login preserves KNOWIT_DB_PATH when patching MCP config for cloud auth
content: The login command should add KNOWIT_CLOUD_TOKEN to patched MCP client configs without deleting KNOWIT_DB_PATH. Removing the DB path breaks the selected project/global source registry context, so the DB path must remain intact even when the user chooses cloud as the default source.

body.text: The login command should add KNOWIT_CLOUD_TOKEN to patched MCP client configs without deleting KNOWIT_DB_PATH. Removing the DB path breaks the selected project/global source registry context, so the DB path must remain intact even when the user chooses cloud as the default source.

title: Cloud login keeps local as the default source unless the user explicitly opts into cloud
content: The login flow no longer implies that authenticating to Knowit Cloud switches all work to cloud automatically. Users are asked whether to make cloud the default source for their current project/global/custom setup, and the default remains local unless they explicitly choose cloud.

body.text: The login flow no longer implies that authenticating to Knowit Cloud switches all work to cloud automatically. Users are asked whether to make cloud the default source for their current project/global/custom setup, and the default remains local unless they explicitly choose cloud.

title: Bugfix release version bumped to 0.2.5
content: After fixing the review-reported issues in the local project checkout, the package version was bumped from 0.2.4 to 0.2.5. This is a patch release because the changes are backward-compatible bugfixes and reliability improvements rather than new user-facing features or breaking changes.

body.text: After fixing the review-reported issues in the local project checkout, the package version was bumped from 0.2.4 to 0.2.5. This is a patch release because the changes are backward-compatible bugfixes and reliability improvements rather than new user-facing features or breaking changes.

title: capture_session_learnings returns partial success instead of failing the whole batch
content: The capture_session_learnings MCP tool now uses Promise.allSettled and returns both stored entries and failure details. A single failed learning no longer prevents the rest of the batch from being persisted.

body.text: The capture_session_learnings MCP tool now uses Promise.allSettled and returns both stored entries and failure details. A single failed learning no longer prevents the rest of the batch from being persisted.

title: Local embedding generation retries transient failures and falls back without embeddings
content: SqliteMemorySource wraps embedding generation with up to two attempts and exponential backoff for retryable failures, then falls back to the non-embedding path. Missing OPENAI_API_KEY is treated as a normal non-retryable fallback and is logged at debug rather than warning to preserve the intended graceful local-first behavior.

body.text: SqliteMemorySource wraps embedding generation with up to two attempts and exponential backoff for retryable failures, then falls back to the non-embedding path. Missing OPENAI_API_KEY is treated as a normal non-retryable fallback and is logged at debug rather than warning to preserve the intended graceful local-first behavior.

title: MemoryService supports explicit database injection
content: MemoryService now accepts an optional constructor object with database, repository, and source-registry dependencies. Singleton-backed defaults remain for normal CLI and MCP usage, but tests and advanced callers can isolate state by injecting a database explicitly.

body.text: MemoryService now accepts an optional constructor object with database, repository, and source-registry dependencies. Singleton-backed defaults remain for normal CLI and MCP usage, but tests and advanced callers can isolate state by injecting a database explicitly.

title: Knowledge entries persist summary in SQLite with compatibility migration
content: Knowledge entries now persist the optional summary field end-to-end. The SQLite schema includes a summary TEXT column, initializeDatabase adds it for legacy databases via compatibility migration, and KnowledgeRepository reads/writes summary on create, update, and fetch.

body.text: Knowledge entries now persist the optional summary field end-to-end. The SQLite schema includes a summary TEXT column, initializeDatabase adds it for legacy databases via compatibility migration, and KnowledgeRepository reads/writes summary on create, update, and fetch.

title: NPM publish requires a fresh semver version
content: Before publishing the knowit package to npm, verify the target version in package.json is newer than the latest npm release. npm will reject republishing an existing version. For this repo, release prep should include bumping package.json and package-lock.json, then running npm test and npm run pack:smoke before npm publish.

body.text: Before publishing the knowit package to npm, verify the target version in package.json is newer than the latest npm release. npm will reject republishing an existing version. For this repo, release prep should include bumping package.json and package-lock.json, then running npm test and npm run pack:smoke before npm publish.

title: Knowit launch messaging risk
content: External messaging should avoid mixing the local-first open-source story with cloud-oriented language unless the cloud path is publicly ready. Current repo surface area includes cloud-related code and URLs, so marketing copy should stay anchored on the publicly available workflow: npm package, MCP server, CLI, SQLite/local-first memory, and agent integration.

body.text: External messaging should avoid mixing the local-first open-source story with cloud-oriented language unless the cloud path is publicly ready. Current repo surface area includes cloud-related code and URLs, so marketing copy should stay anchored on the publicly available workflow: npm package, MCP server, CLI, SQLite/local-first memory, and agent integration.

title: Knowit external positioning for early marketing
content: For early public marketing, position Knowit as a local-first structured memory layer for AI coding agents rather than as a generic AI productivity tool. Lead with the concrete value: agents retrieve durable project context before planning or editing code. The strongest proof point is dogfooding: Knowit uses Knowit during its own development.

body.text: For early public marketing, position Knowit as a local-first structured memory layer for AI coding agents rather than as a generic AI productivity tool. Lead with the concrete value: agents retrieve durable project context before planning or editing code. The strongest proof point is dogfooding: Knowit uses Knowit during its own development.

title: Use Knowit casing in product copy
content: Use the product name casing `Knowit` in user-facing copy, metadata, and landing pages. Avoid `KnowIt`, which conflicts with the package name, README, and repository branding.

body.text: Use the product name casing `Knowit` in user-facing copy, metadata, and landing pages. Avoid `KnowIt`, which conflicts with the package name, README, and repository branding.

title: Knowit positioning against Claude memory and Basic Memory
content: Knowit should be positioned as structured, agent-native engineering memory for teams. Compared with Claude Code memory, Knowit is model/vendor-agnostic, queryable via MCP tools, shareable across teammates through a common SQLite store, and supports structured knowledge types plus source routing. Compared with Basic Memory, Knowit is less about general markdown knowledge graphs and more about operational coding context: rules, architecture, conventions, decisions, repo/domain scoping, installer-managed agent instructions, tiered retrieval, deduplicated session learnings, and routed external canonical sources such as Notion.

body.text: Knowit should be positioned as structured, agent-native engineering memory for teams. Compared with Claude Code memory, Knowit is model/vendor-agnostic, queryable via MCP tools, shareable across teammates through a common SQLite store, and supports structured knowledge types plus source routing. Compared with Basic Memory, Knowit is less about general markdown knowledge graphs and more about operational coding context: rules, architecture, conventions, decisions, repo/domain scoping, installer-managed agent instructions, tiered retrieval, deduplicated session learnings, and routed external canonical sources such as Notion.

title: Benchmark CLI skips update notifier
content: Machine-readable benchmark commands must not emit update-notifier output. The CLI treats the benchmark command family as non-interactive so JSON summaries and CSV generation stay clean for automation.

body.text: Machine-readable benchmark commands must not emit update-notifier output. The CLI treats the benchmark command family as non-interactive so JSON summaries and CSV generation stay clean for automation.

title: Benchmark harness is local-first
content: The benchmark subsystem uses local SQLite-backed Knowit only. Control runs prohibit Knowit use, while treatment runs seed a temporary local Knowit database and require resolve_context before planning.

body.text: The benchmark subsystem uses local SQLite-backed Knowit only. Control runs prohibit Knowit use, while treatment runs seed a temporary local Knowit database and require resolve_context before planning.

title: Tracked .mcp.json is intentional for Knowit self-hosting during development
content: This repository intentionally tracks .mcp.json so Knowit can use Knowit as a development tool while building itself. README and contributing docs should explain that this is repo-specific and not the default recommendation for consumer projects.

body.text: This repository intentionally tracks .mcp.json so Knowit can use Knowit as a development tool while building itself. README and contributing docs should explain that this is repo-specific and not the default recommendation for consumer projects.

title: Keep MCP server version derived from package metadata
content: Knowit should read the MCP server version from package.json instead of hardcoding it, so the CLI/package version and MCP-reported version stay aligned across releases.

body.text: Knowit should read the MCP server version from package.json instead of hardcoding it, so the CLI/package version and MCP-reported version stay aligned across releases.

title: Use package files negation to exclude build tests from npm tarballs
content: When the package needs dist/ for runtime but not dist/tests/, exclude test artifacts in package.json files with explicit negation patterns and verify with npm pack --dry-run.

body.text: When the package needs dist/ for runtime but not dist/tests/, exclude test artifacts in package.json files with explicit negation patterns and verify with npm pack --dry-run.

title: Open source launch should ignore all local env files except examples
content: For public release hygiene, .gitignore should exclude broad local environment files such as .env.* and keep only committed examples like .env.example to reduce accidental secret commits.

body.text: For public release hygiene, .gitignore should exclude broad local environment files such as .env.* and keep only committed examples like .env.example to reduce accidental secret commits.

title: Installer-generated MCP config in repo needs an explicit decision
content: Tracking .mcp.json in the repository preconfigures Knowit for contributors and can mask the fresh-install path. Keep it only if that behavior is explicitly intended and documented; otherwise let knowit install generate it per project.

body.text: Tracking .mcp.json in the repository preconfigures Knowit for contributors and can mask the fresh-install path. Keep it only if that behavior is explicitly intended and documented; otherwise let knowit install generate it per project.

title: Open source launch checks should include MCP version sync
content: Before releasing Knowit, keep the MCP server version in src/server/mcpServer.ts aligned with the package/CLI version in package.json and src/cli/index.ts to avoid capability/version drift in clients.

body.text: Before releasing Knowit, keep the MCP server version in src/server/mcpServer.ts aligned with the package/CLI version in package.json and src/cli/index.ts to avoid capability/version drift in clients.

title: Launch README should optimize for npm scanning
content: The package README should lead with a one-line value proposition, install command, quick start, concrete CLI examples, MCP tools, configuration, contribution links, and a clear launch-status note. This keeps the npm page scannable and avoids burying the setup path under narrative copy.

body.text: The package README should lead with a one-line value proposition, install command, quick start, concrete CLI examples, MCP tools, configuration, contribution links, and a clear launch-status note. This keeps the npm page scannable and avoids burying the setup path under narrative copy.

title: CLI defaults to local-first while cloud is paused
content: The public npm package should not enable cloud access by default. MemoryService skips cloud source bootstrapping unless KNOWIT_CLOUD_ENABLED=true, and cloud-facing CLI commands such as login, whoami, and migrate-to-cloud return a clear local-first launch message when cloud is disabled.

body.text: The public npm package should not enable cloud access by default. MemoryService skips cloud source bootstrapping unless KNOWIT_CLOUD_ENABLED=true, and cloud-facing CLI commands such as login, whoami, and migrate-to-cloud return a clear local-first launch message when cloud is disabled.

title: Public launch keeps cloud access disabled by default
content: Cloud access is gated behind KNOWIT_CLOUD_ENABLED and should remain unavailable in the public local-first release unless explicitly enabled. Local shared knowledge should not store private cloud rollout or access-program details.

body.text: Cloud access is gated behind KNOWIT_CLOUD_ENABLED and should remain unavailable in the public local-first release unless explicitly enabled. Local shared knowledge should not store private cloud rollout or access-program details.

title: Cloud availability gating is enforced server-side
content: Cloud availability and entitlement checks must be enforced on the server. UI gating is only a convenience layer and must not be treated as an authority for access decisions.

body.text: Cloud availability and entitlement checks must be enforced on the server. UI gating is only a convenience layer and must not be treated as an authority for access decisions.

title: Semantic and fallback cloud retrieval honor repo and domain filters
content: Knowit Cloud semantic search RPCs and text fallbacks must preserve tenant, repo, and domain filters. knowledge.resolve should fall back to filtered text retrieval instead of returning an empty result set when embeddings are unavailable or fail.

body.text: Knowit Cloud semantic search RPCs and text fallbacks must preserve tenant, repo, and domain filters. knowledge.resolve should fall back to filtered text retrieval instead of returning an empty result set when embeddings are unavailable or fail.

title: Team invite acceptance is bound to the invited email
content: A team invite may only be accepted by a signed-in account whose email matches the invite email case-insensitively. Invalid, expired, already accepted, and email-mismatch invite states must fail without creating membership. Accepted invites should record both accepted_at and accepted_by_account_id.

body.text: A team invite may only be accepted by a signed-in account whose email matches the invite email case-insensitively. Invalid, expired, already accepted, and email-mismatch invite states must fail without creating membership. Accepted invites should record both accepted_at and accepted_by_account_id.

title: Team membership defaults cloud knowledge operations to team tenant
content: For Knowit Cloud, tenant selection should consistently prefer the team tenant when the authenticated account has an active team membership; otherwise operations use the individual account tenant. This rule applies consistently to supported cloud authentication paths.

body.text: For Knowit Cloud, tenant selection should consistently prefer the team tenant when the authenticated account has an active team membership; otherwise operations use the individual account tenant. This rule applies consistently to supported cloud authentication paths.

title: Knowit Cloud credentials must not override an explicit non-cloud default source
content: When Knowit boots with saved cloud credentials, it should register the cloud source without forcing it to become the default if the user has already chosen a non-cloud default such as local or a routed source like Notion. Explicit local or routed installs must keep their selected default source across restarts; only explicit cloud env configuration or an existing local/cloud default may promote cloud to default automatically.

body.text: When Knowit boots with saved cloud credentials, it should register the cloud source without forcing it to become the default if the user has already chosen a non-cloud default such as local or a routed source like Notion. Explicit local or routed installs must keep their selected default source across restarts; only explicit cloud env configuration or an existing local/cloud default may promote cloud to default automatically.

title: Open-source package exposes API contract only
content: The open-source knowit npm package is a client. It may expose client wiring and stable cloud API shapes, but private entitlement rules, quota rules, monetization logic, database details, and rollout details must stay out of the package and out of local shared knowledge. Rule: never add client-side cloud entitlement feature flags to the open-source package; authoritative access checks belong on the server.

body.text: The open-source knowit npm package is a client. It may expose client wiring and stable cloud API shapes, but private entitlement rules, quota rules, monetization logic, database details, and rollout details must stay out of the package and out of local shared knowledge. Rule: never add client-side cloud entitlement feature flags to the open-source package; authoritative access checks belong on the server.

title: Supabase TypeScript type workaround for ssr/supabase-js version mismatch
content: @supabase/ssr@0.6.1 was compiled against a 3-param SupabaseClient; @supabase/supabase-js@2.99.x has 5 params — the 3rd arg maps to SchemaName (not Schema), causing Schema=never and all table types resolving to never. Fix: cast createServerClient() and createBrowserClient() return values as SupabaseClient<Database> in src/lib/supabase/server.ts and client.ts. Also add explicit CookieOptionsWithName type annotation on the setAll cookie callback parameter. Do not upgrade @supabase/ssr without verifying the generic param count matches.

body.text: @supabase/ssr@0.6.1 was compiled against a 3-param SupabaseClient; @supabase/supabase-js@2.99.x has 5 params — the 3rd arg maps to SchemaName (not Schema), causing Schema=never and all table types resolving to never. Fix: cast createServerClient() and createBrowserClient() return values as SupabaseClient<Database> in src/lib/supabase/server.ts and client.ts. Also add explicit CookieOptionsWithName type annotation on the setAll cookie callback parameter. Do not upgrade @supabase/ssr without verifying the generic param count matches.

title: Knowit MCP instruction check and startup warning
content: src/server/instructionCheck.ts checks for <!-- knowit:start --> marker in CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md at project, .claude/, home dir, and .codex/ locations. If not found, mcpServer.ts calls server.sendLoggingMessage({ level: "warning", data: INSTRUCTIONS_WARNING }) after connect. The list_sources tool also prepends the warning to its response (only list_sources — not all tools — to avoid noise). registerTools() accepts instructionsInstalled=true as a third param. This ensures agents and users are prompted to install instructions if they have not run knowit install.

body.text: src/server/instructionCheck.ts checks for <!-- knowit:start --> marker in CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md at project, .claude/, home dir, and .codex/ locations. If not found, mcpServer.ts calls server.sendLoggingMessage({ level: "warning", data: INSTRUCTIONS_WARNING }) after connect. The list_sources tool also prepends the warning to its response (only list_sources — not all tools — to avoid noise). registerTools() accepts instructionsInstalled=true as a third param. This ensures agents and users are prompted to install instructions if they have not run knowit install.

title: Knowit web dashboard route protection and auth state
content: Knowit web dashboard routes should require authentication before rendering account-specific cloud surfaces. Any gated cloud capability shown in the dashboard must also be enforced by the API layer; client-side navigation and locked UI states are not sufficient access control.

body.text: Knowit web dashboard routes should require authentication before rendering account-specific cloud surfaces. Any gated cloud capability shown in the dashboard must also be enforced by the API layer; client-side navigation and locked UI states are not sufficient access control.

title: Knowit tiered retrieval — two-phase search
content: Implemented OpenViking-inspired tiered retrieval (migration 004_tiered_retrieval.sql): Phase 1 tools (search_knowledge, resolve_context) return title + summary + metadata + score — no full content. Phase 2 tool (get_knowledge) fetches full content by IDs. summary column added to knowledge_entries: regular text column, auto-populated by trigger as left(content, 300) on INSERT if not provided; agents can supply a hand-written summary at store time (max 300 chars) for higher quality. search_knowledge_semantic RPC updated to return summary instead of content. store endpoint does upsert by natural key (title + type + scope + repo + domain + tenant) — matching entry is updated in-place, no duplicates across sessions. capture_session_learnings automatically inherits upsert behavior. matchedOn field added to results: "semantic" or "text". get_knowledge available on both SQLite and cloud providers. MemorySourceProvider interface extended with optional getKnowledge?(ids: string[]). knowledgeResultSchema now has content optional (Phase 1 omits it).

body.text: Implemented OpenViking-inspired tiered retrieval (migration 004_tiered_retrieval.sql): Phase 1 tools (search_knowledge, resolve_context) return title + summary + metadata + score — no full content. Phase 2 tool (get_knowledge) fetches full content by IDs. summary column added to knowledge_entries: regular text column, auto-populated by trigger as left(content, 300) on INSERT if not provided; agents can supply a hand-written summary at store time (max 300 chars) for higher quality. search_knowledge_semantic RPC updated to return summary instead of content. store endpoint does upsert by natural key (title + type + scope + repo + domain + tenant) — matching entry is updated in-place, no duplicates across sessions. capture_session_learnings automatically inherits upsert behavior. matchedOn field added to results: "semantic" or "text". get_knowledge available on both SQLite and cloud providers. MemorySourceProvider interface extended with optional getKnowledge?(ids: string[]). knowledgeResultSchema now has content optional (Phase 1 omits it).

title: Knowit cloud database hardening
content: Knowit cloud database work should preserve tenant isolation, avoid dynamic SQL where static typed queries are possible, bound stored content and in-process caches, and enforce quotas server-side. Local shared knowledge should capture these security principles without storing private quota values, schema details, or rollout-specific limits.

body.text: Knowit cloud database work should preserve tenant isolation, avoid dynamic SQL where static typed queries are possible, bound stored content and in-process caches, and enforce quotas server-side. Local shared knowledge should capture these security principles without storing private quota values, schema details, or rollout-specific limits.

title: Private cloud details stay out of local shared memory
content: Do not store private cloud architecture, token formats, entitlement rules, access-program details, or rollout mechanics in local shared Knowit memory. Keep local entries limited to public-safe product boundaries and security principles; private cloud business details belong in the private cloud repository or source of record.

body.text: Do not store private cloud architecture, token formats, entitlement rules, access-program details, or rollout mechanics in local shared Knowit memory. Keep local entries limited to public-safe product boundaries and security principles; private cloud business details belong in the private cloud repository or source of record.

title: Markdown clutter reduction is a core value proposition
content: Knowit should be positioned as a way to keep durable engineering memory out of repositories. README, landing page, and onboarding copy should explicitly frame Knowit as replacing scattered AGENTS.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, PATTERNS.md, PRDs, and ADR-style documents when those files are being used as long-lived agent memory.

body.text: Knowit should be positioned as a way to keep durable engineering memory out of repositories. README, landing page, and onboarding copy should explicitly frame Knowit as replacing scattered AGENTS.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, PATTERNS.md, PRDs, and ADR-style documents when those files are being used as long-lived agent memory.

title: Manual Init Is Advanced Path
content: `knowit init` should be documented and described as the low-level manual storage bootstrap, not the default onboarding path. User-facing setup should lead with `knowit install` instead.

body.text: `knowit init` should be documented and described as the low-level manual storage bootstrap, not the default onboarding path. User-facing setup should lead with `knowit install` instead.

title: Install Is The Primary Onboarding Path
content: `knowit install` is now the primary setup flow. It initializes the local database automatically when needed, then handles client registration, instruction-file updates, optional project `.mcp.json` generation, and markdown migration. `knowit init` remains as a low-level manual storage bootstrap.

body.text: `knowit install` is now the primary setup flow. It initializes the local database automatically when needed, then handles client registration, instruction-file updates, optional project `.mcp.json` generation, and markdown migration. `knowit init` remains as a low-level manual storage bootstrap.

title: No-Import Case Should Not Prompt
content: If the installer detects no importable markdown knowledge files, it should continue automatically and print a note instead of prompting the user. Prompting in the no-op case creates unnecessary friction and a confusing default choice.

body.text: If the installer detects no importable markdown knowledge files, it should continue automatically and print a note instead of prompting the user. Prompting in the no-op case creates unnecessary friction and a confusing default choice.

title: Agent Instruction Files Are Not Markdown Migration Targets
content: AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md are operational instruction files managed in place by the installer. They must not be imported into Knowit as migrated knowledge documents, because that would duplicate the instruction layer into the knowledge base.

body.text: AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md are operational instruction files managed in place by the installer. They must not be imported into Knowit as migrated knowledge documents, because that would duplicate the instruction layer into the knowledge base.

title: Project MCP Config Uses Global Knowit Command
content: Project-scoped MCP configuration should use the globally installed `knowit serve` entrypoint, not a local dist path from the current working copy. The generated .mcp.json uses command `knowit`, args `["serve"]`, and a repo-relative `.knowit/knowit.db` path so the config is portable across machines.

body.text: Project-scoped MCP configuration should use the globally installed `knowit serve` entrypoint, not a local dist path from the current working copy. The generated .mcp.json uses command `knowit`, args `["serve"]`, and a repo-relative `.knowit/knowit.db` path so the config is portable across machines.

title: Routed defaults fall back to local for direct Knowit operations
content: When a routed source such as Notion is marked as the default source, source-optional direct Knowit operations must still use the local SQLite source. This applies to both writes like storeKnowledge and direct read APIs such as listKnowledge, getKnowledgeEntry, and getKnowledgeStats. resolveSourceAction remains the path that should prefer the routed default for canonical external artifacts.

body.text: When a routed source such as Notion is marked as the default source, source-optional direct Knowit operations must still use the local SQLite source. This applies to both writes like storeKnowledge and direct read APIs such as listKnowledge, getKnowledgeEntry, and getKnowledgeStats. resolveSourceAction remains the path that should prefer the routed default for canonical external artifacts.

title: Installer Imports Common Knowledge Markdown Files
content: The install workflow can migrate common knowledge markdown files such as AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, PRD.md, and ADR-style documents into Knowit. Imported files are stored as repo-scoped local knowledge entries with source-path metadata, and the installer does not delete the original files.

body.text: The install workflow can migrate common knowledge markdown files such as AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, PRD.md, and ADR-style documents into Knowit. Imported files are stored as repo-scoped local knowledge entries with source-path metadata, and the installer does not delete the original files.

title: Routed Source Default Uses Local Storage Fallback
content: If a routed source like Notion is marked as the default source, direct Knowit storage still falls back to the local SQLite source. This keeps store_knowledge and CLI writes functional while allowing resolve_source_action to prefer the routed provider for plans, PRDs, and other durable artifacts.

body.text: If a routed source like Notion is marked as the default source, direct Knowit storage still falls back to the local SQLite source. This keeps store_knowledge and CLI writes functional while allowing resolve_source_action to prefer the routed provider for plans, PRDs, and other durable artifacts.

title: Interactive Install Command Implementation
content: Knowit now implements a first-class interactive `knowit install` command. The installer prompts for target clients, install scope, preferred source, and optional markdown migration, then applies MCP registration plus instruction-file updates in one workflow.

body.text: Knowit now implements a first-class interactive `knowit install` command. The installer prompts for target clients, install scope, preferred source, and optional markdown migration, then applies MCP registration plus instruction-file updates in one workflow.

title: Source Discovery And Validation Plan
content: Knowit should eventually discover configured MCP servers from supported clients, detect whether a provider like Notion is already installed, and validate that a routed source points at a usable downstream MCP. This should support future commands such as source discovery, provider connection, and source doctor validation.

body.text: Knowit should eventually discover configured MCP servers from supported clients, detect whether a provider like Notion is already installed, and validate that a routed source points at a usable downstream MCP. This should support future commands such as source discovery, provider connection, and source doctor validation.

title: Routed Source Execution Model
content: When the selected source is external, Knowit should return structured routing guidance that tells the agent which downstream MCP to use, plus static read and write hints. The user should only need to mention Knowit; the agent should resolve the downstream provider through Knowit rather than requiring the user to name Notion or another tool explicitly.

body.text: When the selected source is external, Knowit should return structured routing guidance that tells the agent which downstream MCP to use, plus static read and write hints. The user should only need to mention Knowit; the agent should resolve the downstream provider through Knowit rather than requiring the user to name Notion or another tool explicitly.

title: Static Routing Metadata
content: Read and write hints for routed sources must be static deterministic metadata generated from source configuration, not LLM-authored text. Routing behavior needs to be testable, consistent, and independent of model output.

body.text: Read and write hints for routed sources must be static deterministic metadata generated from source configuration, not LLM-authored text. Routing behavior needs to be testable, consistent, and independent of model output.

title: Durable Docs Default To Knowit Memory
content: When users ask an agent to create plans, PRDs, architecture notes, or durable documentation and they invoke Knowit, the default behavior should be to consult Knowit first and then either store the artifact in the local source or route to the configured external provider. Repo-local markdown should be created only when the user explicitly asks for a file.

body.text: When users ask an agent to create plans, PRDs, architecture notes, or durable documentation and they invoke Knowit, the default behavior should be to consult Knowit first and then either store the artifact in the local source or route to the configured external provider. Repo-local markdown should be created only when the user explicitly asks for a file.

title: Client-Level Instructions Drive Knowit Adoption
content: Making Knowit available as an MCP is not enough to guarantee use. Supported clients need project-scoped or global instructions that tell agents to consult Knowit before planning or documentation work and to store durable learnings back into Knowit.

body.text: Making Knowit available as an MCP is not enough to guarantee use. Supported clients need project-scoped or global instructions that tell agents to consult Knowit before planning or documentation work and to store durable learnings back into Knowit.

title: Knowit Install Scopes
content: `knowit install` should support both global and project scopes. Global install configures a client once for all repos, while project install writes or updates project-local instructions so Knowit becomes the default memory layer only inside that codebase.

body.text: `knowit install` should support both global and project scopes. Global install configures a client once for all repos, while project install writes or updates project-local instructions so Knowit becomes the default memory layer only inside that codebase.

title: Knowit Install Must Preserve Existing Config
content: Client installation should merge or append to existing client instructions and MCP config rather than overwriting user content. Knowit needs a detect-preview-apply flow so users can see what will change before it edits client configuration.

body.text: Client installation should merge or append to existing client instructions and MCP config rather than overwriting user content. Knowit needs a detect-preview-apply flow so users can see what will change before it edits client configuration.

title: Client-Aware Instruction Templates
content: Knowit should ship client-specific instruction templates for tools like Claude Code and Codex instead of a single generic block. The install workflow should render the right template for each client and scope, because config locations and instruction formats differ across tools.

body.text: Knowit should ship client-specific instruction templates for tools like Claude Code and Codex instead of a single generic block. The install workflow should render the right template for each client and scope, because config locations and instruction formats differ across tools.

title: Preferred Source Connection UX
content: The main user-facing source onboarding flow should be a limited set of known providers such as `local`, `notion`, and future first-class integrations. Raw `source add-mcp` remains available as an advanced escape hatch, but it should not be the primary UX for normal users.

body.text: The main user-facing source onboarding flow should be a limited set of known providers such as `local`, `notion`, and future first-class integrations. Raw `source add-mcp` remains available as an advanced escape hatch, but it should not be the primary UX for normal users.

title: Knowit Install Command
content: Knowit should provide a first-class `knowit install` workflow that configures both MCP connectivity and client-level instructions for supported AI tools. The goal is to make Knowit the default memory layer without forcing users to manually edit client config files.

body.text: Knowit should provide a first-class `knowit install` workflow that configures both MCP connectivity and client-level instructions for supported AI tools. The goal is to make Knowit the default memory layer without forcing users to manually edit client config files.

title: Knowit Retrieval Behavior
content: OpenAI embeddings are an optional retrieval enhancement, not a storage prerequisite. Local storage and retrieval must continue working without OPENAI_API_KEY, falling back to text and tag matching when embeddings are unavailable.

body.text: OpenAI embeddings are an optional retrieval enhancement, not a storage prerequisite. Local storage and retrieval must continue working without OPENAI_API_KEY, falling back to text and tag matching when embeddings are unavailable.

title: Knowit Current Public Interface
content: The current public surface includes a serve command for the stdio MCP server, CLI commands for init/add/search/list/show/stats/resolve, source list/show/connect/add-mcp, and MCP tools including list_sources, connect_source, store_knowledge, search_knowledge, resolve_context, resolve_source_action, and capture_session_learnings.

body.text: The current public surface includes a serve command for the stdio MCP server, CLI commands for init/add/search/list/show/stats/resolve, source list/show/connect/add-mcp, and MCP tools including list_sources, connect_source, store_knowledge, search_knowledge, resolve_context, resolve_source_action, and capture_session_learnings.

title: Knowit Current Product State
content: Knowit is currently a CLI and MCP product with no GUI yet. Configuration is environment-driven, and the README currently teaches client registration plus adding AGENTS.md or CLAUDE.md instructions so agents resolve context before work and capture learnings after a session.

body.text: Knowit is currently a CLI and MCP product with no GUI yet. Configuration is environment-driven, and the README currently teaches client registration plus adding AGENTS.md or CLAUDE.md instructions so agents resolve context before work and capture learnings after a session.

title: Knowit Version Mismatch
content: The repository currently has version drift: package.json is at 0.2.2 while the CLI and MCP server code report 0.2.1. This is a small but real release hygiene issue that can confuse users about what version is actually running.

body.text: The repository currently has version drift: package.json is at 0.2.2 while the CLI and MCP server code report 0.2.1. This is a small but real release hygiene issue that can confuse users about what version is actually running.

title: Knowit Preferred Source Onboarding
content: The product-facing source onboarding flow is connect_source, not raw MCP registration. Known providers such as local and notion should be connected through a guided path that creates either a local source or a routed provider record with setup, read, and write hints. register_mcp_source remains the advanced escape hatch for explicit custom MCP tool mapping.

body.text: The product-facing source onboarding flow is connect_source, not raw MCP registration. Known providers such as local and notion should be connected through a guided path that creates either a local source or a routed provider record with setup, read, and write hints. register_mcp_source remains the advanced escape hatch for explicit custom MCP tool mapping.

title: Knowit Storage Scope Model
content: The local SQLite store now has an explicit storage scope model. Project scope stores at .knowit/knowit.db in the current repo, global scope stores at ~/.knowit/knowit.db, and custom scope uses KNOWIT_DB_PATH. This path resolution lives outside the SQLite source record itself because the database path must be known before the database can be opened.

body.text: The local SQLite store now has an explicit storage scope model. Project scope stores at .knowit/knowit.db in the current repo, global scope stores at ~/.knowit/knowit.db, and custom scope uses KNOWIT_DB_PATH. This path resolution lives outside the SQLite source record itself because the database path must be known before the database can be opened.

title: Knowit Shared Database Pattern
content: Team-shared memory is implemented by pointing multiple users or clients at the same SQLite database path. The recommended setup is a shared KNOWIT_DB_PATH for team memory, while repo-local project installs default to .knowit/knowit.db.

body.text: Team-shared memory is implemented by pointing multiple users or clients at the same SQLite database path. The recommended setup is a shared KNOWIT_DB_PATH for team memory, while repo-local project installs default to .knowit/knowit.db.

title: Knowit Knowledge Metadata Rules
content: Repo-scoped and domain-scoped knowledge must include repo metadata, and domain-scoped knowledge must also include a domain. This is important for retrieval quality and to avoid cross-repo ambiguity when multiple repositories share one database.

body.text: Repo-scoped and domain-scoped knowledge must include repo metadata, and domain-scoped knowledge must also include a domain. This is important for retrieval quality and to avoid cross-repo ambiguity when multiple repositories share one database.

title: Knowit Runtime Boundary
content: MemoryService is the core orchestration boundary. CLI commands and MCP tools call MemoryService, which routes operations through SourceRepository and SourceRegistry to provider implementations. Provider-specific behavior belongs in sqlite/mcp source classes rather than being embedded in CLI or MCP tool handlers.

body.text: MemoryService is the core orchestration boundary. CLI commands and MCP tools call MemoryService, which routes operations through SourceRepository and SourceRegistry to provider implementations. Provider-specific behavior belongs in sqlite/mcp source classes rather than being embedded in CLI or MCP tool handlers.

title: Knowit Source Model
content: Knowit currently supports three source kinds: sqlite for the built-in local knowledge base, mcp for explicit external MCP-backed knowledge sources, and route for provider guidance records such as Notion. Route sources are not directly searchable/storable through provider classes; they exist to tell the agent which downstream MCP to use next and whether distilled memory should also be stored back in Knowit.

body.text: Knowit currently supports three source kinds: sqlite for the built-in local knowledge base, mcp for explicit external MCP-backed knowledge sources, and route for provider guidance records such as Notion. Route sources are not directly searchable/storable through provider classes; they exist to tell the agent which downstream MCP to use next and whether distilled memory should also be stored back in Knowit.

title: Knowit Product Definition
content: Knowit is shared team memory for AI coding agents. It exposes an MCP server and CLI that let agents store, search, and resolve persistent project knowledge across sessions and teammates instead of restarting from zero every time.

body.text: Knowit is shared team memory for AI coding agents. It exposes an MCP server and CLI that let agents store, search, and resolve persistent project knowledge across sessions and teammates instead of restarting from zero every time.
